statement: No autonomous Iliad orchestrator or service exists. 'The Iliad' names the registered lifecycle umbrella — a coordination discipline realized by records (enrollment, pre-reviews, retrospectives) and their verifier checks, not a deployed runtime. Same rule as Argo and Daedalus.

Enrolling a sub-system under the umbrella does not absorb it into the TELOS spine or change its governance: spine work stays under TELOS authorization; products stay deferred until a deliberate AM-40-class amendment. Enrollment adds lifecycle records, not authority.

The verifier proves registered enrollments are complete and honest; it CANNOT mechanically detect an implementation that never registered. That gap is closed by review discipline (an unenrolled delivery should fail human review), not by the oracle.

The pre-review records model INTENT (env-resolved seat ids, new models considered); it does not and cannot verify external provider catalogs. Which model actually answered is proven downstream by per-seat provenance in the run records.

A retrospective's optimizations bind nothing by themselves — each is adopted (or deferred with a reason) by a future run's pre-review, or graduates into a reviewed data/contract change. The record closes the loop; humans and the gate still decide.
